behavior_summary: This script implements the 'U8P_SKELETON::look' (examine) handler for a Skeleton actor. When an actor (typically the player) inspects the skeleton, the script marks the skeleton with a short info state (0x0208) and excludes it from normal processing, then makes the skeleton 'bark' the literal text "skeleton ".

The bark call's return value is captured and used in a short implies/pop sequence, after which the calling process is suspended. The net gameplay effect is to show or vocalize the skeleton's description to the player during an examination interaction and then stop further immediate processing of the skeleton's script.
notable_control_flow: Entry on an examination trigger: Event 0 ('U8P_SKELETON::look') starts U8P_SKELETON::look.
Set info and process exclude: script writes a per-process info key (literal 0x0208) and requests exclusion from processing (likely to prevent immediate re-processing or change state).
Emit description: constructs the fixed string "skeleton " and calls Item::bark with the actor PID to present that text to the player.

Return-value handling: captures the return value of Item::bark, performs an 'implies' style operation (push retval, implies, pop into a temp), then suspends the process. The implies/pop sequence appears to influence control flow or postpone further reactions based on the bark result.
open_questions: Exact semantic meaning of info id 0x0208 and what 'process exclude' covers (short-term exclusion from processing vs. a persistent state change) — inferred but not explicit (confidence: medium).
What the return value of Item::bark represents in-game (acknowledgment, error, queued action) and how the implies/pop sequence uses it to change subsequent behavior (confidence: low).
Whether suspending the process is intended to fully halt AI for this actor until an external resume, or is a short-term suspend used by the engine for timing/dialog flow (confidence: medium).

main_function.name: U8P_SKELETON::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_SKELETON::look' (examine) event for a skeleton actor: set a small info/exclusion state on the actor and emit the literal description "skeleton " to the viewer, then process the bark return value and suspend the actor's process.
entry_conditions: Event 0 (U8P_SKELETON::look) — invoked when the player or relevant actor inspects or looks at the skeleton (enters the skeleton's U8P_SKELETON::look/observe trigger area).

function_map.name: U8P_SKELETON::look
function_map.role: Examination handler for the Skeleton actor (process-based event handler).

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine/item method used to present or vocalize a short text string from an item/actor to a target process/player.

external_calls.purpose: Make the skeleton actor emit/display the literal string "skeleton " to the viewing process/player.
